courses.name: Foundational Myths and Histories
courses.description: Recurring symbols in myth, epic and ritual representing the relation between the sacred and the profane, the origin of the cosmos, the basis of community, and formative human experiences. Primary sources drawn from ancient India and China, Mesopotamia, the Hebrew Bible, and Indigenous cultures.
courses.prerequisites: restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Humanities and Classical Civilization
courses.description: The ideas which animated ancient Greek and Roman civilization and which influenced later western cultural movements through a reading of literary, historical, and philosophical works. Authors include Homer, Herodotus, Thucydides, the Greek Tragedians, Plato, Vergil, and Cicero.
courses.prerequisites: restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Classical Literature and Its Reception
courses.description: The study of different types of ancient literature and the reception of Classical works in later periods. A focus on writing a research essay.
courses.prerequisites: restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Introduction to the Humanities: Great Books - Big Ideas
courses.description: An introduction to major ideas in the Humanities, through some of the most influential and exciting texts from Antiquity to the present day. Works may include the Bible, the Bhagavad Gita, Homer’s Odyssey, Plato’s Republic, Dante’s Inferno, Machiavelli’s The Prince, Shakespeare’s Hamlet, Shelley’s Frankenstein.
courses.prerequisites: Students in the Bachelor of Humanities are not eligible to register in this course.

courses.name: Reason and Revelation
courses.description: The origins of philosophy in ancient Greece and its pursuit in the medieval West, with special attention to knowledge, happiness, and love. Readings include works by Plato, Aristotle, Plotinus, Augustine, Boethius, Aquinas, and Dante.
courses.prerequisites: HUMS 1000 and enrolment  in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Art from Antiquity to the Medieval World
courses.description: A chronological and thematic survey of the Arts from the earliest times to ca. 1400.
courses.prerequisites: restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Modern European Art 1527-2000
courses.description: A chronological and thematic survey of the Arts from the sixteenth to the twenty-first century.
courses.prerequisites: HUMS 2101 and restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Culture and Imagination
courses.description: Major forms of literary, artistic, and philosophical expression from 1500-1800. Sources drawn from renaissance humanism, reformation theology, enlightenment and romantic philosophy.
courses.prerequisites: HUMS 2000 and enrolment in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Western Music 1000-1850
courses.description: Introduction to basic theory, harmony, history and interpretation of Western music including the Medieval, Renaissance, Baroque, Classical and early Romantic periods.
courses.prerequisites: restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Western Music 1850-2000
courses.description: Western music from the mid-nineteenth century to the present with emphasis on the seminal contributions of Liszt, Wagner, Mahler, Debussy, Stravinsky, Schönberg and others.
courses.prerequisites: HUMS 3102 and restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: European Literature
courses.description: Major movements and works from Dante's Divine Comedy through Voltaire's Candide. Themes include the New Humanism vs. old Chivalry in the Renaissance and Baroque periods; the rise of the modern novel and drama; reason, nature, and the Enlightenment project.
courses.prerequisites: HUMS 2000 and third-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Ancient and Medieval Intellectual History
courses.description: Examination of some of the major philosophical, religious, political, artistic, and/or literary ideas, works, and movements from Archaic Greece to the High Middle Ages.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Renaissance and Early Modern Intellectual History
courses.description: Examination of some of the major philosophical, religious, political, artistic, and/or literary ideas, works, and movements from the Early Renaissance to 1800.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Humanities in Context
courses.description: Designed for students studying humanities, this travel course explores art, literature, politics, philosophy, architecture, religions, and cultures in their historical and contemporary contexts in a particular geographic locale. Travel destinations and themes vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: 2.0 credits in HUMS and permission of the department. Permission of the unit is required to repeat this course.

courses.name: Politics, Modernity and the Common Good
courses.description: Modern and post-modern ways of thinking and doing, including revolutionary new ideas in politics, philosophy, culture, economics, and international relations. Thinkers considered include Arendt, Foucault, Hegel, Heidegger, Hobbes, Kant, Marx, Nietzsche, Polanyi, Rousseau, Said, and Taylor.
courses.prerequisites: HUMS3000 and enrolment in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Directed Studies in the Humanities
courses.description: A course for independent study and writing, under the supervision of a College designated faculty member. This course involves supervised readings and written essays.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Directed Studies in the Humanities
courses.description: A course for independent study and writing, under the supervision of a College designated faculty member. This course involves supervised readings and written essays.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program and Good Standing in the program.

courses.name: Science in the Modern World
courses.description: An introduction to the major scientific ideas of our time (such as Big Bang theory, molecular genetics, evolution, atomic structure), and the impact of technology on society (e.g. global warming, pollution, genetically modified foods, viral infections).
courses.prerequisites: restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Modern Intellectual History
courses.description: Examination of some of the major ideas and ideologies from 1800 to the present, including romanticism, liberalism, nationalism, symbolism, socialism, Freudianism, communism, feminism, and postmodernism.
courses.prerequisites: restricted to students in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Research Seminar: Antiquity to the Middle Ages
courses.description: An interdisciplinary seminar on a selected topic in the humanities from Antiquity to the Middle Ages. The topic will vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Research Seminar: Renaissance to Enlightenment
courses.description: An interdisciplinary seminar on a selected topic in the humanities from the Renaissance to the Enlightenment. The topic will vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Research Seminar: Romanticism to the Present
courses.description: An interdisciplinary seminar on a selected topic in the humanities from Romanticism to the present. The topic will vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program.

courses.name: Research Seminar: Non-Western Traditions
courses.description: An interdisciplinary seminar on a selected topic in the humanities as expressed in aboriginal and Non-Western cultures. The topic will vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Humanities program.
